artifacts.description: Run log documenting source searches, inspection steps, and why no valid smoking-prevalence estimate for Asian teens could be computed from the retrieved materials.
Table summarizing each candidate source checked in this run, its relevance, and why it was insufficient for original estimation.

limitations: No machine-readable dataset containing smoking outcomes for teenagers stratified specifically for Asian youth was successfully retrieved in this run.
The CDC search results that were accessible included an infographic landing page and adult smoking material, but not a directly usable teen subgroup table for original estimation.
The GitHub mirror hint available in the prompt pointed to a BRFSS diabetes dataset, which is not appropriate for estimating smoking prevalence among Asian teens.
Python in this environment could not directly fetch external URLs, so analysis depended on the web retrieval tool; without a downloadable tabular file exposed there, computation was not possible.
Because meaningful original estimation was not possible with the materials actually retrieved, returning a numeric prevalence would risk overclaiming.
methods: I translated the theme into a concrete empirical question about smoking prevalence among Asian teenagers. I then searched CDC-hosted data sources for youth smoking datasets with race/ethnicity stratification, using queries focused on youth tobacco use, YRBS, NYTS, and Asian race/ethnicity subgroups. I inspected the returned CDC pages and evaluated whether they provided machine-readable data sufficient to compute an original prevalence estimate for Asian teens in this run. I also checked the user-provided GitHub mirror hint and determined it referred to a BRFSS 2015 diabetes dataset, which is unrelated to smoking among Asian teenagers. Because I could not retrieve a usable tabular dataset with teen-by-Asian subgroup smoking counts or denominators from the available sources in this run, I did not fabricate an estimate. I saved a retrieval log and a source-check table documenting the failed-but-reproducible feasibility assessment.
research_question: Can the prevalence of cigarette smoking among Asian teenagers be estimated from the user-provided dataset hints and directly retrievable public data sources available in this run?

sources.label: CDC Data search result: Youth And Tobacco Use Infographic
sources.notes: Topic-relevant CDC page was found, but in this run it did not yield a machine-readable teen-by-Asian subgroup dataset suitable for direct prevalence estimation.

api_query.search: site:data.cdc.gov cigarette smoking race ethnic disparities adults

sources.label: CDC Data search result: Race and Ethnic Disparities in Cigarette Smoking Among Adults
sources.notes: Accessible CDC result concerned adults rather than teenagers, so it was not suitable for the study question.

sources.label: GitHub repository hint: BRFSS 2015 diabetes dataset mirror
sources.notes: Repository description indicates BRFSS 2015 diabetes health indicators files, which are unrelated to smoking prevalence among Asian teens.

title: Feasibility assessment for estimating smoking prevalence among Asian teens with the provided materials
